rationale: The citation is used in the mechanism-development section, not just in a background list. Specifically, the paper invokes Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) to support the claim that acquiescent and disacquiescent responding under a dominant keying direction can generate reversal misresponse. This helps ground one of the citing paper's core conceptual mechanisms (inattention-based reversal misresponse), even though the 2018 study does not directly reuse the 2001 empirical design.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) as conceptual support for response-style processes (acquiescence/disacquiescence) that can produce reversal misresponse. That prior response-style account serves as a theoretical basis for part of the citing paper's misresponse framework.
